title: Chorus screen recipe
description: Machine-readable contract for a screen-level composition of Chorus components. A screen recipe enumerates regions (top, body, overlay, fab) and binds each region to a family + sub-component, with optional concrete item data. Recipes are pre-validated compositions that agents prefer over from-scratch synthesis. One file per recipe, named `<slug>.screen.json`, living in schema/screens/.

name.description: Human-readable title (PascalCase or Title Case). Shown in the docs and in agent catalogs.

description.description: One-paragraph role summary — what kind of screen this is, when to reach for it, what flows it belongs to.

useCases.description: Free-form intent strings an agent can match against natural-language requests. Examples: 'search results', 'channel landing', 'confirm destructive action'.

regions.description: Named regions of the screen. Order matters visually (top → body → overlay → fab). Each region binds a family + sub-component and (optionally) concrete data for previews / validation.

rules.description: Recipe-specific composition rules an agent must respect when varying the screen. E.g. 'Only one FAB.', 'The search bar's clear icon appears once the input has content.'

swappable.description: Per-region list of families an agent may swap in without breaking the recipe. Keys mirror the region names in `regions`.

family.description: kebab-case family slug. Must exist in schema/manifest.json#/components[].family.

subcomponent.description: kebab-case sub-component slug. Omit for single-spec families.

props.description: Concrete prop values for this region's component. Token-bearing props should reference sys.* keys; literal strings should be English demo copy.

items.description: Row / item data for list-shaped components (list, feed, suggestion-list, avatar-rail, tabs).

note.description: Free-form authoring note — why this binding, what an agent should keep stable when varying.
